records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.226 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
‘Whoever believes in Allah (swt) and the Last Day should not enter the bathhouse except with a waistcloth (covering).’
And He prohibited washing under the open sky without a covering and prohibited entering rivers without a covering, saying: 'The water has inhabitants and dwellers.'
records.arabicText: 226 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: « «مَنْ كٰانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللّٰهِ وَ اَلْيَوْمِ اَلْآخِرِ» فَلاَ يَدْخُلِ اَلْحَمَّامَ إِلاَّ بِمِئْزَرٍ » وَ نَهَى صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَنِ اَلْغُسْلِ تَحْتَ اَلسَّمَاءِ إِلاَّ بِمِئْزَرٍ وَ نَهَى عَنْ دُخُولِ اَلْأَنْهَارِ إِلاَّ بِمِئْزَرٍ فَقَالَ «إِنَّ لِلْمَاءِ أَهْلاً وَ سُكَّاناً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.226 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
‘Whoever believes in Allah (swt) and the Last Day should not enter the bathhouse except with a waistcloth (covering).’
And He prohibited washing under the open sky without a covering and prohibited entering rivers without a covering, saying: 'The water has inhabitants and dwellers.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.227 - Al-Hasan ibn Musa ibn Jafar narrated from his mother and Umm Ahmad bint Musa (as) who said:
"We were both with Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) in the desert while we were heading to Baghdad, and on Thursday He (as) said to us,
'Perform ghusl today for tomorrow, Jumu’ah, because water will be scarce tomorrow.'
They said: So we performed ghusl on Thursday for Jumu’ah.
records.arabicText: 227 - فَقَدْ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ أُمِّهِ وَ أُمِّ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَتَا: كُنَّا مَعَ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي اَلْبَادِيَةِ وَ نَحْنُ نُرِيدُ بَغْدَادَ فَقَالَ لَنَا يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ «اِغْتَسِلاَ اَلْيَوْمَ لِغَدٍ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فَإِنَّ اَلْمَاءَ غَداً بِهَا قَلِيلٌ» قَالَتَا فَاغْتَسَلْنَا يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ لِلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.227 - Al-Hasan ibn Musa ibn Jafar narrated from his mother and Umm Ahmad bint Musa (as) who said:
"We were both with Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) in the desert while we were heading to Baghdad, and on Thursday He (as) said to us,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'Perform ghusl today for tomorrow, Jumu’ah, because water will be scarce tomorrow.'
They said: So we performed ghusl on Thursday for Jumu’ah.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.229 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
'The ghusl (ritual bath) of Jumu’ah is a purification and an expiation for the sins committed between one Jumu’ah and the next (Jumu’ah).'
records.arabicText: 229 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «غُسْلُ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ طَهُورٌ وَ كَفَّارَةٌ لِمَا بَيْنَهُمَا مِنَ اَلذُّنُوبِ مِنَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ إِلَى اَلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.229 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'The ghusl (ritual bath) of Jumu’ah is a purification and an expiation for the sins committed between one Jumu’ah and the next (Jumu’ah).'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.230 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said regarding the reason for the Jumu’ah (ritual bath):
'The Ansar (residents of Medina who supported the Prophet) used to work in their fields and with their livestock. When Jumu’ah came, they would attend the mosque, and the people were bothered by the odor from their armpits and bodies. So the Messenger of Allah (sw) ordered them to perform ghusl, and thus it became a Sunnah (established practice).'
records.arabicText: 230 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي عِلَّةِ غُسْلِ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ «إِنَّ اَلْأَنْصَارَ كَانَتْ تَعْمَلُ فِي نَوَاضِحِهَا وَ أَمْوَالِهَا فَإِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ اَلْجُمُعَةِ حَضَرُوا اَلْمَسْجِدَ فَتَأَذَّى اَلنَّاسُ بِأَرْوَاحِ آبَاطِهِمْ وَ أَجْسَادِهِمْ فَأَمَرَهُمْ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِالْغُسْلِ فَجَرَتْ بِذَلِكَ اَلسُّنَّةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.230 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said regarding the reason for the Jumu’ah (ritual bath):
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'The Ansar (residents of Medina who supported the Prophet) used to work in their fields and with their livestock. When Jumu’ah came, they would attend the mosque, and the people were bothered by the odor from their armpits and bodies. So the Messenger of Allah (sw) ordered them to perform ghusl, and thus it became a Sunnah (established practice).'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.231 – (Al-Hussain ibn Khalid asked Abu Al-Hasan Al-Awwal (as)) narrates that:
‘It is narrated that 'Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, completes the obligatory prayer with the supererogatory prayer, completes the obligatory fasting with the supererogatory fasting, and completes the wudu (ablution) with the ghusl (ritual bath) of Jumu’ah.’.
records.arabicText: 231 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى أَتَمَّ صَلاَةَ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ بِصَلاَةِ اَلنَّافِلَةِ وَ أَتَمَّ صِيَامَ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ بِصِيَامِ اَلنَّافِلَةِ وَ أَتَمَّ اَلْوُضُوءَ بِغُسْلِ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.231 – (Al-Hussain ibn Khalid asked Abu Al-Hasan Al-Awwal (as)) narrates that:
records.thaqalaynMatn: ‘It is narrated that 'Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, completes the obligatory prayer with the supererogatory prayer, completes the obligatory fasting with the supererogatory fasting, and completes the wudu (ablution) with the ghusl (ritual bath) of Jumu’ah.’.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.232 - Yahya ibn Sa'id Al-Ahwazi narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr, and him from Muhammad ibn Hamran who reported that Imam Ja'far Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When you enter the bathhouse (hammam), at the time you remove your clothes, say:
Allāhumma inzi‘ ‘annī ribqata al-nifāq wa thabbitnī ‘ala al-īmān
'O’ Allah (swt), remove from me the shackles of hypocrisy and establish me firmly upon faith.'
And when you enter the first room (of bathhouse), say:
Allāhumma innī a‘ūdhu bika min sharri nafsī wa asta‘īdhu bika min adhāh
'O’ Allah (swt), I seek refuge with You from the evil of my soul and seek Your protection from its harm.'"
And when you enter the second room (of bathhouse), say:
Allāhumma adhhib ‘annī al-rijsa al-najsa wa ṭahhir jasadī wa qalbī
'O’ Allah (swt), remove impurity and defilement from me, and purify my body and heart.'
Take some warm water and pour it over your head and pour it over your feet. If you can drink a sip of it, then do so, as it cleanses the bladder. Remain in the second room for a while.
When you enter the third room (of bathhouse), say:
Na‘ūdhu billāhi mina al-nār wa nas’aluhu al-jannah
'We seek refuge in Allah (swt) from the Fire and ask Him for Paradise,' and repeat it until the time you leave the hot room (of bathhouse).
Be careful not to drink cold water or consuming ‘fuqqa'a’ (like carbonated drinks) in the bathhouse, as it harms the stomach.
Do not pour cold water over yourself, as it weakens the body. However, pouring cold water on your feet when leaving will drive away illness from your body.
When you put on your clothes, say:
Allāhumma albisnī al-taqwā wa jannibnī al-radā
'O’ Allah (swt), clothe me with piety and protect me from ruin.'
If you do this, you will be safe from all ailments.'
records.arabicText:
232 - وَ رَوَى يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ اَلْأَهْوَازِيُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حُمْرَانَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا دَخَلْتَ اَلْحَمَّامَ فَقُلْ فِي اَلْوَقْتِ اَلَّذِي تَنْزِعُ فِيهِ ثِيَابَكَ: اَللَّهُمَّ اِنْزِعْ عَنِّي رِبْقَةَ اَلنِّفَاقِ وَ ثَبِّتْنِي عَلَى اَلْإِيمَانِ
وَ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ اَلْبَيْتَ اَلْأَوَّلَ فَقُلْ: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَ أَسْتَعِيذُ بِكَ مِنْ أَذَاهُ
وَ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ اَلْبَيْتَ اَلثَّانِيَ فَقُلِ: اَللَّهُمَّ أَذْهِبْ عَنِّي اَلرِّجْسَ اَلنِّجْسَ وَ طَهِّرْ جَسَدِي وَ قَلْبِي
وَ خُذْ مِنَ اَلْمَاءِ اَلْحَارِّ وَ ضَعْهُ عَلَى هَامَتِكَ وَ صُبَّ مِنْهُ عَلَى رِجْلَيْكَ وَ إِنْ أَمْكَنَ أَنْ تَبْلَعَ مِنْهُ جُرْعَةً فَافْعَلْ فَإِنَّهُ يُنَقِّي اَلْمَثَانَةَ وَ اِلْبَثْ فِي اَلْبَيْتِ اَلثَّانِي سَاعَةً
وَ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ اَلْبَيْتَ اَلثَّالِثَ فَقُلْ نَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ اَلنَّارِ وَ نَسْأَلُهُ اَلْجَنَّةَ تُرَدِّدُهَا إِلَى وَقْتِ خُرُوجِكَ مِنَ اَلْبَيْتِ اَلْحَارِّ وَ إِيَّاكَ وَ شُرْبَ اَلْمَاءِ اَلْبَارِدِ وَ اَلْفُقَّاعِ فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ فَإِنَّهُ يُفْسِدُ اَلْمَعِدَةَ
وَ لاَ تَصُبَّنَّ عَلَيْكَ اَلْمَاءَ اَلْبَارِدَ فَإِنَّهُ يُضْعِفُ اَلْبَدَنَ وَ صُبَّ اَلْمَاءَ اَلْبَارِدَ عَلَى قَدَمَيْكَ إِذَا خَرَجْتَ فَإِنَّهُ يَسُلُّ اَلدَّاءَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ فَإِذَا لَبِسْتَ ثِيَابَكَ فَقُلِ: اَللَّهُمَّ أَلْبِسْنِي اَلتَّقْوَى وَ جَنِّبْنِي اَلرَّدَى فَإِذَا فَعَلْتَ ذَلِكَ أَمِنْتَ مِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.232 - Yahya ibn Sa'id Al-Ahwazi narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Nasr, and him from Muhammad ibn Hamran who reported that Imam Ja'far Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When you enter the bathhouse (hammam), at the time you remove your clothes, say:
Allāhumma inzi‘ ‘annī ribqata al-nifāq wa thabbitnī ‘ala al-īmān
'O’ Allah (swt), remove from me the shackles of hypocrisy and establish me firmly upon faith.'
And when you enter the first room (of bathhouse), say:
Allāhumma innī a‘ūdhu bika min sharri nafsī wa asta‘īdhu bika min adhāh
'O’ Allah (swt), I seek refuge with You from the evil of my soul and seek Your protection from its harm.'"
And when you enter the second room (of bathhouse), say:
Allāhumma adhhib ‘annī al-rijsa al-najsa wa ṭahhir jasadī wa qalbī
'O’ Allah (swt), remove impurity and defilement from me, and purify my body and heart.'
Take some warm water and pour it over your head and pour it over your feet. If you can drink a sip of it, then do so, as it cleanses the bladder. Remain in the second room for a while.
When you enter the third room (of bathhouse), say:
Na‘ūdhu billāhi mina al-nār wa nas’aluhu al-jannah
'We seek refuge in Allah (swt) from the Fire and ask Him for Paradise,' and repeat it until the time you leave the hot room (of bathhouse).
Be careful not to drink cold water or consuming ‘fuqqa'a’ (like carbonated drinks) in the bathhouse, as it harms the stomach.
Do not pour cold water over yourself, as it weakens the body. However, pouring cold water on your feet when leaving will drive away illness from your body.
When you put on your clothes, say:
Allāhumma albisnī al-taqwā wa jannibnī al-radā
'O’ Allah (swt), clothe me with piety and protect me from ruin.'
If you do this, you will be safe from all ailments.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.233 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) saying:
'Did The Commander of the Faithful (as) prohibit the recitation of the Qur'an in the bathhouse?'
Imam (as) said: 'No, he only prohibited a man from reciting while being naked. If he is wearing a waistcloth (covering), then there is no harm.'
records.arabicText: 233 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ: أَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَنْهَى عَنْ قِرَاءَةِ اَلْقُرْآنِ فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ فَقَالَ «لاَ إِنَّمَا نَهَى أَنْ يَقْرَأَ اَلرَّجُلُ وَ هُوَ عُرْيَانٌ فَإِذَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِ إِزَارٌ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.233 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'Did The Commander of the Faithful (as) prohibit the recitation of the Qur'an in the bathhouse?'
Imam (as) said: 'No, he only prohibited a man from reciting while being naked. If he is wearing a waistcloth (covering), then there is no harm.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.234 - Ali ibn Yaqtin said to Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
'May I recite (in the bathhouse) and marry (perform a marriage contract) there?'
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm.'
records.arabicText: 234 - وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ يَقْطِينٍ لِمُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: أَقْرَأُ فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ وَ أَنْكِحُ فِيهِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.234 - Ali ibn Yaqtin said to Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'May I recite (in the bathhouse) and marry (perform a marriage contract) there?'
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.235 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the saying of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their private parts; that is purer for them." (Surah An-Nur, 24:30).
Imam (as) said: 'Every mention of guarding the private parts in the Book of Allah (swt), the Exalted, refers to guarding against fornication, except in this instance, where it refers to guarding them from being looked at.'
records.arabicText:
235 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: «۝ قُلْ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَغُضُّوا مِنْ أَبْصٰارِهِمْ وَ يَحْفَظُوا فُرُوجَهُمْ ذٰلِكَ أَزْكىٰ لَهُمْ ۝»
فَقَالَ «كُلُّ مَا كَانَ فِي كِتَابِ اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى مِنْ ذِكْرِ حِفْظِ اَلْفَرْجِ فَهُوَ مِنَ اَلزِّنَا إِلاَّ فِي هَذَا اَلْمَوْضِعِ فَإِنَّهُ لِلْحِفْظِ مِنْ أَنْ يُنْظَرَ إِلَيْهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.235 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the saying of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their private parts; that is purer for them." (Surah An-Nur, 24:30).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'Every mention of guarding the private parts in the Book of Allah (swt), the Exalted, refers to guarding against fornication, except in this instance, where it refers to guarding them from being looked at.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.236 - On the authority of Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"Indeed, looking at the private parts of a Muslim is disliked, and looking at the private parts of a non-Muslim is like looking at the private parts of a donkey."
records.arabicText: 236 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «إِنَّمَا أَكْرَهُ اَلنَّظَرَ إِلَى عَوْرَةِ اَلْمُسْلِمِ فَأَمَّا اَلنَّظَرُ إِلَى عَوْرَةِ مَنْ لَيْسَ بِمُسْلِمٍ مِثْلُ اَلنَّظَرِ إِلَى عَوْرَةِ اَلْحِمَارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.236 - On the authority of Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, looking at the private parts of a Muslim is disliked, and looking at the private parts of a non-Muslim is like looking at the private parts of a donkey."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.237 - The Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
'What a good place the bathhouse is, where one is reminded of the Fire (Hellfire) and it removes filth.'
records.arabicText: 237 - وَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «نِعْمَ اَلْبَيْتُ اَلْحَمَّامُ تُذَكَّرُ فِيهِ اَلنَّارُ وَ يَذْهَبُ بِالدَّرَنِ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.238 - Imam (as) said:
'The bathhouse is an undesirable place; it removes coverings (privacy) and takes away modesty.'
records.arabicText: 238 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «بِئْسَ اَلْبَيْتُ اَلْحَمَّامُ يَهْتِكُ اَلسِّتْرَ وَ يَذْهَبُ بِالْحَيَاءِ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.239 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
'The bathhouse is an undesirable place; it removes coverings (privacy) and exposes the private parts. However, it is a good place as it reminds one of the heats of the Fire (Hellfire).'.
records.arabicText: 239 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «بِئْسَ اَلْبَيْتُ اَلْحَمَّامُ يَهْتِكُ اَلسِّتْرَ وَ يُبْدِي اَلْعَوْرَةَ وَ نِعْمَ اَلْبَيْتُ اَلْحَمَّامُ يُذَكِّرُ حَرَّ اَلنَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.239 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'The bathhouse is an undesirable place; it removes coverings (privacy) and exposes the private parts. However, it is a good place as it reminds one of the heats of the Fire (Hellfire).'.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.240 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Whoever believes in Allah (swt) and the Last Day (Qiyamah) should not send his wife to the bathhouse."
records.arabicText: 240 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: « «مَنْ كٰانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللّٰهِ وَ اَلْيَوْمِ اَلْآخِرِ» فَلاَ يَبْعَثْ بِحَلِيلَتِهِ إِلَى اَلْحَمَّامِ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Whoever believes in Allah (swt) and the Last Day (Qiyamah) should not send his wife to the bathhouse."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.241 - Imam (as) said: 'Whoever obeys his wife (in matters of disobedience) will be thrown by Allah (swt), the Exalted, on his face in the Fire.'
It was said: 'What kind of obedience is that?'
Imam (as) said: "When She invites him to go to congregation of lamentations to extravagant festivities (weddings), or going to public bathhouses, and or to wear delicate (thin/revealing) clothing; and he approves it for her."
records.arabicText: 241 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ أَطَاعَ اِمْرَأَتَهُ أَكَبَّهُ اَللَّهُ عَلَى مَنْخِرَيْهِ فِي اَلنَّارِ » فَقِيلَ وَ مَا تِلْكَ اَلطَّاعَةُ قَالَ «تَدْعُوهُ إِلَى اَلنِّيَاحَاتِ وَ اَلْعُرُسَاتِ وَ اَلْحَمَّامَاتِ وَ لُبْسِ اَلثِّيَابِ اَلرِّقَاقِ فَيُجِيبُهَا.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.242 - Abu Basir asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who neglects the Jumu’ah ritual bath (ghusl) either forgetfully or intentionally.
Imam (as) said: 'If he was forgetful, his prayer is valid. But if he did so intentionally, then he should seek forgiveness from Allah (swt) and not repeat it.’
records.arabicText: 242 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَدَعُ غُسْلَ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ نَاسِياً أَوْ مُتَعَمِّداً فَقَالَ «إِذَا كَانَ نَاسِياً فَقَدْ تَمَّتْ صَلاَتُهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مُتَعَمِّداً فَلْيَسْتَغْفِرِ اَللَّهَ وَ لاَ يَعُدْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.242 - Abu Basir asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who neglects the Jumu’ah ritual bath (ghusl) either forgetfully or intentionally.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: 'If he was forgetful, his prayer is valid. But if he did so intentionally, then he should seek forgiveness from Allah (swt) and not repeat it.’

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.243 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
'Do not recline in the bathhouse, for it melts the fat around the kidneys.
Do not comb your hair in the bathhouse, for it weakens the hair.
Do not wash your head with clay, for it disfigures the face,' and in another narration, 'it removes protective modesty (ghayrah).
Do not scrub with pottery shards (khuzfah), for it causes leprosy,
And do not wipe your face with the izar (waistcloth), for it removes the glamour of the face.
And it is narrated that this refers to the clay of Egypt and the khuzfah (pottery) of Sham."
records.arabicText:
243 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ تَتَّكِ فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ فَإِنَّهُ يُذِيبُ شَحْمَ اَلْكُلْيَتَيْنِ وَ لاَ تُسَرِّحْ فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ فَإِنَّهُ يُرَقِّقُ اَلشَّعْرَ وَ لاَ تَغْسِلْ رَأْسَكَ بِالطِّينِ فَإِنَّهُ يُسَمِّجُ اَلْوَجْهَ» وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ «يَذْهَبُ بِالْغَيْرَةِ وَ لاَ تَدَلَّكْ بِالْخَزَفِ فَإِنَّهُ يُورِثُ اَلْبَرَصَ وَ لاَ تَمْسَحْ وَجْهَكَ بِالْإِزَارِ فَإِنَّهُ يَذْهَبُ بِمَاءِ اَلْوَجْهِ. وَ رُوِيَ «أَنَّ ذَلِكَ طِينُ مِصْرَ وَ خَزَفُ اَلشَّامِ.
والسواك في الحمام يورث وباء الأسنان. ولا يجوز التطهير والغسل بغسالة الحمام.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.244 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
'Let each of you adorn himself on Jumu’ah; perform the ritual bath (ghusl), apply perfume, comb your hair, and wear your cleanest clothes. He should prepare for Jumu’ah and embody tranquility and dignity on that day.
He should enhance worship your Lord (azj) excellently and do as much good as you can, for surely Allah (swt), the Exalted, looks upon the earth to multiply the good deeds (on Jumu’ah)."
records.arabicText: 244 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لِيَتَزَيَّنَنَّ أَحَدُكُمْ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ وَ يَغْتَسِلُ وَ يَتَطَيَّبُ وَ يَتَسَرَّحُ وَ يَلْبَسُ أَنْظَفَ ثِيَابِهِ وَ لْيَتَهَيَّأْ لِلْجُمُعَةِ وَ لْيَكُنْ عَلَيْهِ فِي ذَلِكَ اَلْيَوْمِ اَلسَّكِينَةُ وَ اَلْوَقَارُ وَ لْيُحْسِنْ عِبَادَةَ رَبِّهِ وَ لْيَفْعَلِ اَلْخَيْرَ مَا اِسْتَطَاعَ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ يَطَّلِعُ عَلَى اَلْأَرْضِ لِيُضَاعِفَ اَلْحَسَنَاتِ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.245 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
'Do not enter the bathhouse on an empty stomach, and do not enter it until you have eaten something.'
records.arabicText: 245 - وَقَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ تَدْخُلُوا اَلْحَمَّامَ عَلَى اَلرِّيقِ وَ لاَ تَدْخُلُوهُ حَتَّى تَطْعَمُوا شَيْئاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.245 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'Do not enter the bathhouse on an empty stomach, and do not enter it until you have eaten something.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.246 - Some have narrated: "Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) exited the bathhouse, dressed, and wrapped a turban upon his head.
Imam (as) said: 'I never leave off wearing a turban upon exiting the bathhouse, whether in winter or summer.'
records.arabicText: 246 - وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: خَرَجَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مِنَ اَلْحَمَّامِ فَلَبِسَ وَ تَعَمَّمَ قَالَ «فَمَا تَرَكْتُ اَلْعِمَامَةَ عِنْدَ خُرُوجِي مِنَ اَلْحَمَّامِ فِي اَلشِّتَاءِ وَ اَلصَّيْفِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.246 - Some have narrated: "Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) exited the bathhouse, dressed, and wrapped a turban upon his head.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: 'I never leave off wearing a turban upon exiting the bathhouse, whether in winter or summer.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.247 - Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
'Bathing in the bathhouse every other day does not increase fat. However, using it daily will deplete the fat of the kidneys.'
records.arabicText: 247 - وَقَالَ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْحَمَّامُ يَوْمٌ وَ يَوْمٌ لاَ يُكْثِرُ اَللَّحْمَ وَ إِدْمَانُهُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ يُذْهِبُ شَحْمَ اَلْكُلْيَتَيْنِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.247 - Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'Bathing in the bathhouse every other day does not increase fat. However, using it daily will deplete the fat of the kidneys.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.248 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) would apply Nuura (depilatory paste) in the bathhouse, and when he reached the area of the private parts, he would instruct the one applying it to move away, and then he would apply it himself to that area.
records.arabicText: 248 - وَ كَانَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَطَّلِي فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ فَإِذَا بَلَغَ مَوْضِعَ اَلْعَوْرَةِ قَالَ لِلَّذِي يَطْلِي تَنَحَّ ثُمَّ يَطْلِي هُوَ ذَلِكَ اَلْمَوْضِعَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.248 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) would apply Nuura (depilatory paste) in the bathhouse, and when he reached the area of the private parts,
records.thaqalaynMatn: he would instruct the one applying it to move away, and then he would apply it himself to that area.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.249 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) entered the bathhouse, the bathhouse attendant offered to clear it for him. Imam (as) said: 'No, a believer is light in their burdens.’
records.arabicText: 249 - وَ دَخَلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلْحَمَّامَ فَقَالَ لَهُ صَاحِبُ اَلْحَمَّامِ نُخْلِيهِ لَكَ فَقَالَ «لاَ إِنَّ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ خَفِيفُ اَلْمَئُونَةِ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.250 - It was narrated by Ubaidullah Al-Marafiqi that he entered a bathhouse in Medina and saw an elderly man who was the caretaker of the bathhouse.
(Al-Marafiqi) said to him: ‘O’ Shaykh, 'To whom does this bathhouse belong?'
(Old man) said: ‘It belongs to Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)’
(Al-Marafiqi) asked: 'Did he use to come here?'
(Old man) replied: 'Yes.'
So (Al-Marafiqi) asked: ‘And what does He do (in the bathhouse)?’
(Old man) said: 'He would enter and first anoint the area around his private parts and what was near it. Then, he would wrap his waistcloth (izar) around the edges of his private area and call me to anoint the rest of his body." One day, I said to him, "What you would dislike for me to see, I have already seen.'
Imam (as) said: 'Not at all; the Nuura (depilatory paste) is a form of covering.'
records.arabicText: 250 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اَللَّهِ اَلْمُرَافِقِيِّ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ حَمَّاماً بِالْمَدِينَةِ فَإِذَا شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ وَ هُوَ قَيِّمُ اَلْحَمَّامِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ يَا شَيْخُ لِمَنْ هَذَا اَلْحَمَّامُ فَقَالَ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقُلْتُ أَ كَانَ يَدْخُلُهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ فَقُلْتُ كَيْفَ كَانَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ كَانَ يَدْخُلُ فَيَبْدَأُ فَيَطْلِي عَانَتَهُ وَ مَا يَلِيهَا ثُمَّ يَلُفُّ إِزَارَهُ عَلَى أَطْرَافِ إِحْلِيلِهِ وَ يَدْعُونِي فَأَطْلِي سَائِرَ جَسَدِهِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ يَوْماً مِنَ اَلْأَيَّامِ اَلَّذِي تَكْرَهُ أَنْ أَرَاهُ قَدْ رَأَيْتُهُ قَالَ «كَلاَّ إِنَّ اَلنُّورَةَ سُتْرَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.250 - It was narrated by Ubaidullah Al-Marafiqi that he entered a bathhouse in Medina and saw an elderly man who was the caretaker of the bathhouse.
(Al-Marafiqi) said to him: ‘O’ Shaykh,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'To whom does this bathhouse belong?'
(Old man) said: ‘It belongs to Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)’
(Al-Marafiqi) asked: 'Did he use to come here?'
(Old man) replied: 'Yes.'
So (Al-Marafiqi) asked: ‘And what does He do (in the bathhouse)?’
(Old man) said: 'He would enter and first anoint the area around his private parts and what was near it. Then, he would wrap his waistcloth (izar) around the edges of his private area and call me to anoint the rest of his body." One day, I said to him, "What you would dislike for me to see, I have already seen.'
Imam (as) said: 'Not at all; the Nuura (depilatory paste) is a form of covering.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.251 - Abdur Rahman ibn Muslim, known as Sa'dan, said:
"I was in the middle room of the bathhouse when Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) entered, wearing a cloth over the Nuura (depilatory paste)."
Imam (as) said: 'Peace be upon you,'
I returned the greeting also. Then I entered the room with the basin, performed my ablution, and left.
records.arabicText: 251 - وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ اَلْمَعْرُوفُ بِسَعْدَانَ: كُنْتُ فِي اَلْحَمَّامِ فِي اَلْبَيْتِ اَلْأَوْسَطِ فَدَخَلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ عَلَيْهِ إِزَارٌ فَوْقَ اَلنُّورَةِ فَقَالَ «اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكُمْ» فَرَدَدْتُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمَ وَ دَخَلْتُ اَلْبَيْتَ اَلَّذِي فِيهِ اَلْحَوْضُ فَاغْتَسَلْتُ وَ خَرَجْتُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.251 - Abdur Rahman ibn Muslim, known as Sa'dan, said:
"I was in the middle room of the bathhouse when Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) entered, wearing a cloth over the Nuura (depilatory paste)."
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'Peace be upon you,'
I returned the greeting also. Then I entered the room with the basin, performed my ablution, and left.
